整套首页
unit_18 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_18

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 460 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC DOUBLE
For a genre that's supposed to be about the future, science fiction has certainly (i) __________ lately, (ii) __________ is what sells best, with readers spending their money on sequels to long-running series.For a genre that's supposed to be about the future, science fiction has certainly (i) __________ lately, (ii) __________ is what sells best, with readers spending their money on sequels to long-running series.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　been looking backward; nostalgia
fantasysequelsupposesupposed
fantasy: standard unit headword | sequel: standard unit headword | suppose: standard unit headword | supposed: standard unit headword
#2 SE
The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do __________ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do __________ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.
答案：C, E　succumb to; yield to
shrinkoverreactsuccumb
shrink: standard unit headword | overreact: standard unit headword | succumb: standard unit headword
#3 TC SINGLE
The science of astronomy was begun by amateurs and today remains dependent on their contributions, which are incisive by virtue of being __________ by the a priori assumptions that often vitiate the work of professional research scientists.The science of astronomy was begun by amateurs and today remains dependent on their contributions, which are incisive by virtue of being __________ by the a priori assumptions that often vitiate the work of professional research scientists.
答案：B　unencumbered
a priorivirtuevitiate
a priori: standard unit headword | virtue: standard unit headword | vitiate: standard unit headword
#4 TC TRIPLE
Substantial atmosphere around planetary bodies acts as (i)__________ incoming objects. Smaller objects, particularly those that are lower in density and more fragile, (ii)__________ in the upper reaches of the atmosphere, whereas more intact, larger bodies may survive to impact the surface. Thus, relative to large craters, small craters are much less (iii)__________ on bodies with dense atmosphere, such as Earth, Venus, and Titan, than they are on Mercury and Moon.Substantial atmosphere around planetary bodies acts as (i)__________ incoming objects. Smaller objects, particularly those that are lower in density and more fragile, (ii)__________ in the upper reaches of the atmosphere, whereas more intact, larger bodies may survive to impact the surface. Thus, relative to large craters, small craters are much less (iii)__________ on bodies with dense atmosphere, such as Earth, Venus, and Titan, than they are on Mercury and Moon.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　significant filters to; vaporize; common
denseagglomeratecrater
dense: standard unit headword | agglomerate: standard unit headword | crater: standard unit headword
#5 SE
Individuals interested in longevity have sought to fine-tune their bodies with all kinds of __________ diets: only raw foods, only plants, only the flesh, fruit, and nuts that prehistoric humans would have hunted and foraged.Individuals interested in longevity have sought to fine-tune their bodies with all kinds of __________ diets: only raw foods, only plants, only the flesh, fruit, and nuts that prehistoric humans would have hunted and foraged.
答案：D, F　proscriptive; exacting
finefine-tunelongevity
fine: standard unit headword | fine-tune: standard unit headword | longevity: standard unit headword
#6 TC TRIPLE
An estimated 80 million adults in the United States use some form of alternative medicine, from herbs and megavitamins to yoga and acupuncture. But while (i)__________ claims are made for these treatments, the scientific evidence for them often (ii)__________: studies and clinical trials, when they exist at all, can be (iii)__________ in design and too small to yield reliable insights.An estimated 80 million adults in the United States use some form of alternative medicine, from herbs and megavitamins to yoga and acupuncture. But while (i)__________ claims are made for these treatments, the scientific evidence for them often (ii)__________: studies and clinical trials, when they exist at all, can be (iii)__________ in design and too small to yield reliable insights.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　sweeping; lags far behind; shoddy
alternativelagunfounded
alternative: standard unit headword | lag: standard unit headword | unfounded: standard unit headword
#7 TC DOUBLE
Some dietary experts suggest that we should (i) __________ the traditional notion that low-fat diets are salutary, since, according to these researchers, a low-fat diet has (ii) __________ body weight or general health, and focus instead on the quality of the carbohydrates we are eating.Some dietary experts suggest that we should (i) __________ the traditional notion that low-fat diets are salutary, since, according to these researchers, a low-fat diet has (ii) __________ body weight or general health, and focus instead on the quality of the carbohydrates we are eating.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　jettison; no special benefit for
notionespousedeleterious
notion: standard unit headword | espouse: standard unit headword | deleterious: standard unit headword
#8 TC SINGLE
Many Latin American writers and critics have come to bristle at the very mention of the type of fiction termed “magic realism,” but to the common reader the appeal of such fiction is __________.Many Latin American writers and critics have come to bristle at the very mention of the type of fiction termed “magic realism,” but to the common reader the appeal of such fiction is __________.
答案：B　undiminished
termappeal
term: standard unit headword | appeal: standard unit headword
#9 SE
Whereas ground-based interferometers detect astrophysical events briefly and infrequently, space-based ones should hear a __________ of signals as soon as they turn on, including a constant chorus from binary white dwarfs in our galaxy.Whereas ground-based interferometers detect astrophysical events briefly and infrequently, space-based ones should hear a __________ of signals as soon as they turn on, including a constant chorus from binary white dwarfs in our galaxy.
答案：B, C　plethora; profusion
plethoraprofusion
plethora: standard unit headword | profusion: standard unit headword
#10 TC TRIPLE
For Plato the art of music was so (i)__________ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato's conception seems (ii)__________. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)__________ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i)__________ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato's conception seems (ii)__________. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)__________ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　firmly anchored in; absurd; profound
alterationarticulate
alteration: standard unit headword | articulate: standard unit headword
#11 TC SINGLE
Politicians who invoke the founders of the United States in support of their views seem to imply that the founders consistently concurred in their own views when in reality they were a highly __________ group of thinkers.Politicians who invoke the founders of the United States in support of their views seem to imply that the founders consistently concurred in their own views when in reality they were a highly __________ group of thinkers.
答案：D　contentious
methodicalconcur
methodical: standard unit headword | concur: standard unit headword

答案：A, F　preserving; safeguarding
safeguardrehabilitate

volumeadornment

Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 2
答案：A, D　minimized; tenuous

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　an acquired; advanced; demands

dichotomous

Blank 2
Blank 3

manuscript: standard unit headword
#24 TC DOUBLE
At the heart of present-day studies on computational authorship attribution lies the hypothesis that each author has so personal a writing style that it can function as stylistic DNA. Any given author style, in other words, is regarded as (i)__________ and (ii)__________ every other authors’ writing style.At the heart of present-day studies on computational authorship attribution lies the hypothesis that each author has so personal a writing style that it can function as stylistic DNA. Any given author style, in other words, is regarded as (i)__________ and (ii)__________ every other authors’ writing style.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　stable; distinguishable from
stylistic
stylistic: standard unit headword

答案：A, E　cheerless; gloomy

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　demurred; assuage the qualms
assuage

The nature of our recollections is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

答案：B, F　canned; formulaic
canned

The artist is known for making photographs that deals with political matters, yet because her art is open-ended and evocative, it would be wrong to characterize it as __________.The artist is known for making photographs that deals with political matters, yet because her art is open-ended and evocative, it would be wrong to characterize it as __________.
答案：A　polemical

Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)__________ a population crash. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)__________ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)__________ individual factors and thus (iii)__________ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook
adverse
adverse: standard unit headword

To the avid reader of E.O. Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) __________, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii) __________ and ambition.To the avid reader of E.O. Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) __________, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii) __________ and ambition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　predictable; purview
culmination

Blank 2

presuppose

#48 TC SINGLE
Paintings created in India during the Mughal dynasty were __________ in ambition but ornamental in presentation: in one direction they have an affinity with newspaper photographs, while in the other they have the intricacy of jewels.Paintings created in India during the Mughal dynasty were __________ in ambition but ornamental in presentation: in one direction they have an affinity with newspaper photographs, while in the other they have the intricacy of jewels.
答案：B　documentary
sectarian

#49 SE
Individuals, governments, and companies show ample ability to __________ themselves by setting goals based on current conditions and then blindly following them even when those conditions change drastically.Individuals, governments, and companies show ample ability to __________ themselves by setting goals based on current conditions and then blindly following them even when those conditions change drastically.
答案：A, E　hamstring; impair
hamstring

#50 TC DOUBLE
Some norms governing authors' dedications of their works are (i) __________ the norms governing ordinary speech (it may be wrong to lie, and thus wrong to lie in a dedication), but other norms are (ii) __________ dedications. For example, one ought not dedicate to a worthy recipient an object that one feels is devoid of value.Some norms governing authors' dedications of their works are (i) __________ the norms governing ordinary speech (it may be wrong to lie, and thus wrong to lie in a dedication), but other norms are (ii) __________ dedications. For example, one ought not dedicate to a worthy recipient an object that one feels is devoid of value.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　indistinct from; particular to
dedication
dedication: standard unit headword
#51 TC SINGLE
The film was a __________: its elements were assembled more or less haphazardly from a dozen of different sources.The film was a __________: its elements were assembled more or less haphazardly from a dozen of different sources.
答案：C　pastiche
burlesque
burlesque: standard unit headword
#52 TC TRIPLE
Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)__________ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)__________ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　limitation; decreased over time; a decoupling of
decouple
decouple: standard unit headword
#53 SE
Although it is premature to say whether nanomotors would __________ conventional methods, Wang believes that the most promising applications for nanomotors lie in fast drug delivery or low-cost cleanup of environmental pollutants.Although it is premature to say whether nanomotors would __________ conventional methods, Wang believes that the most promising applications for nanomotors lie in fast drug delivery or low-cost cleanup of environmental pollutants.
答案：D, E　supplant; supersede
premature
premature: standard unit headword

The macromolecule RNA is common to all living beings, and DNA, which is found in all organisms except some bacteria, is almost as __________.The macromolecule RNA is common to all living beings, and DNA, which is found in all organisms except some bacteria, is almost as __________.
答案：C　ubiquitous
exiguous
exiguous: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　umbrage; collectively overthrow

#56 TC TRIPLE
Movement from bottom to top, from poor to rich, was rare; even movement from poor to middle class was (i)__________. Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, "yielded rather (ii)__________ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America." So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)__________ if only the public schools did a better job.Movement from bottom to top, from poor to rich, was rare; even movement from poor to middle class was (i)__________. Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, "yielded rather (ii)__________ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America." So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)__________ if only the public schools did a better job.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　an anomaly; pessimistic; obliterated
obliterate
obliterate: standard unit headword
#57 SE
In matters of taste, the art patron and collector Peggy Guggenheim was __________: she was for the strangest, the most surprising, the most satisfying, the best, the unique.In matters of taste, the art patron and collector Peggy Guggenheim was __________: she was for the strangest, the most surprising, the most satisfying, the best, the unique.
答案：D, F　an extremist; a zealot

Not all paleontologists agree that connections between the continents were (i)__________ just after the extinction of the dinosaurs. Some hold the view that North America, Asia, and South America had (ii)__________ immediately following the dinosaur extinction, pointing to (iii)__________ between ancient kinds of mammals that existed on all three continents at this time in support of their argument.Not all paleontologists agree that connections between the continents were (i)__________ just after the extinction of the dinosaurs. Some hold the view that North America, Asia, and South America had (ii)__________ immediately following the dinosaur extinction, pointing to (iii)__________ between ancient kinds of mammals that existed on all three continents at this time in support of their argument.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　limited; extensive contacts; similarities

lasting: standard unit headword

Archaeopteryx had feathers on its hind limbs that helped stabilize it and acted as air brakes, allowing the otherwise __________ creature to turn more adeptly and fly steadily.Archaeopteryx had feathers on its hind limbs that helped stabilize it and acted as air brakes, allowing the otherwise __________ creature to turn more adeptly and fly steadily.
答案：B　ungainly

Blank 2
答案：A, F　abstruse; general

Admittedly, biography by its very nature is (iii) __________ a life--an intimate and possibly unflattering examination by the biographer, who burrows deeper and deeper to reveal the unseen and illuminate the unexpected--but unauthorized biographies can be a public service and a boon to future historians.

Therefore, lying (iii) __________ cognitive development and social skills in a way that honesty simply does not.Given children's active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as (i) __________ virtue in young children, but it turns out that lying is the more (ii) __________ skill. Blank 2

答案：B, D, I　an acquired; advanced; demands

As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

For a philosopher of the analytic tradition, Williams' approach is somewhat (i) __________. Unlike most analytic philosophers, Williams has not kept himself (ii) __________ the rest of the humanities; in fact, he wishes to address colleagues in other fields in terms that will make sense to them. In particular, he objects to the (iii) __________ character of analytic philosophy: the amount it tries to accomplish by conceptual analysis and a priori argument alone. Williams is convinced that philosophy must use history, including historical imagination, to understand and defend values of any kind.For a philosopher of the analytic tradition, Williams' approach is somewhat (i) __________. Unlike most analytic philosophers, Williams has not kept himself (ii) __________ the rest of the humanities; in fact, he wishes to address colleagues in other fields in terms that will make sense to them. In particular, he objects to the (iii) __________ character of analytic philosophy: the amount it tries to accomplish by conceptual analysis and a priori argument alone. Williams is convinced that philosophy must use history, including historical imagination, to understand and defend values of any kind.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　unorthodox; aloof from; strongly ahistorical

For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated

surreptitious
surreptitious: standard unit headword
#101 SE
There are great __________ in countries’ greenhouse gas emissions, especially in per capita terms; while the United States and China are similar in aggregate emissions, United States’ per capita emissions are a huge multiple of China’s.There are great __________ in countries’ greenhouse gas emissions, especially in per capita terms; while the United States and China are similar in aggregate emissions, United States’ per capita emissions are a huge multiple of China’s.
答案：B, E　disparities; variances
term
term: standard unit headword
#102 TC TRIPLE
The modern iron suspension bridge dates from the early 19th century, but it did not have (i)__________ debut: many early suspension bridges were damaged, if not outright destroyed, by the wind. There were few (ii)__________, however, so the form (iii)__________.The modern iron suspension bridge dates from the early 19th century, but it did not have (i)__________ debut: many early suspension bridges were damaged, if not outright destroyed, by the wind. There were few (ii)__________, however, so the form (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　a propitious; practical alternatives; persisted
alternative
alternative: standard unit headword
#103 TC SINGLE
History teaches us that science is not __________ enterprise; indeed, it is quite the opposite, a motley assortment of tools designed to safeguard researchers against their own biases.History teaches us that science is not __________ enterprise; indeed, it is quite the opposite, a motley assortment of tools designed to safeguard researchers against their own biases.
答案：E　a monolithic
safeguard
safeguard: standard unit headword
#104 TC DOUBLE
Evidence suggests that populations of migratory birds in both the New and Old Worlds have (i)__________, a (ii)__________ that carries the potential for significant ecological damage far beyond the shrinking numbers of the birds themselves.Evidence suggests that populations of migratory birds in both the New and Old Worlds have (i)__________, a (ii)__________ that carries the potential for significant ecological damage far beyond the shrinking numbers of the birds themselves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　plummeted; decline
shrink
shrink: standard unit headword
#105 SE
Many innovative breakthroughs come about when people venture beyond their own areas of expertise, because often it takes an outsider to ask the naïve question that may yield __________ solution.Many innovative breakthroughs come about when people venture beyond their own areas of expertise, because often it takes an outsider to ask the naïve question that may yield __________ solution.
答案：B, F　an unconventional; an original
venture
venture: standard unit headword
#106 TC DOUBLE
He was never (i)__________: he was nothing if not (ii)__________, so he forbore for the present to declare his passion.He was never (i)__________: he was nothing if not (ii)__________, so he forbore for the present to declare his passion.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　impetuous; circumspect
circumspect
circumspect: standard unit headword
#107 TC SINGLE
Even if he wants to serve again—and given his obvious love for the job, the assumption among insiders is that he is more likely to stay than go—there is at least one __________ his serving another term.Even if he wants to serve again—and given his obvious love for the job, the assumption among insiders is that he is more likely to stay than go—there is at least one __________ his serving another term.
答案：A　impediment to
term
term: standard unit headword
#108 TC TRIPLE
The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)__________ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)__________—he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)__________ force in the development and production of a movie.The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)__________ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)__________—he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)__________ force in the development and production of a movie.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　enduring; profit mongers; creative
notion
notion: standard unit headword
#109 SE
Noise suppression in phones can play an important role in making cellphone networks more efficient, since when sounds that are __________ to the meaningful signal are transmitted, precious network bandwidth is wasted.Noise suppression in phones can play an important role in making cellphone networks more efficient, since when sounds that are __________ to the meaningful signal are transmitted, precious network bandwidth is wasted.
答案：C, F　irrelevant; extraneous
extraneous
extraneous: standard unit headword
#110 TC SINGLE
Consolidating a memory is not instantaneous, after all, or even __________: every memory must be encoded and moved from short-term to long-term storage and some of those memories are, for whatever reason, more vividly imprinted than others.Consolidating a memory is not instantaneous, after all, or even __________: every memory must be encoded and moved from short-term to long-term storage and some of those memories are, for whatever reason, more vividly imprinted than others.
答案：E　inevitable
term
term: standard unit headword
#111 TC DOUBLE
People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water-cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentment if they were less (i)__________ about their problem and initiated a more (ii)__________ dialogue.People frequently attempt to relieve their workplace frustrations via surreptitious comments around the water-cooler but would be better able to resolve their resentment if they were less (i)__________ about their problem and initiated a more (ii)__________ dialogue.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　clandestine; open
surreptitious
surreptitious: standard unit headword
#112 TC TRIPLE
Behavioral economists have come to believe that a (i)__________ of choices can be paralyzing as Schwartz pointed out in the recent book The Paradox of Choice. Studies of retirement plans show that the more investment choices a plan offers, the less likely people are to participate in it. It may follow, then, that a lack of flexibility in certain plans may actually be a (ii)__________. People reasonably (iii)__________ some advantages in exchange for peace of mind.Behavioral economists have come to believe that a (i)__________ of choices can be paralyzing as Schwartz pointed out in the recent book The Paradox of Choice. Studies of retirement plans show that the more investment choices a plan offers, the less likely people are to participate in it. It may follow, then, that a lack of flexibility in certain plans may actually be a (ii)__________. People reasonably (iii)__________ some advantages in exchange for peace of mind.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　surfeit; virtue; forgo
virtue
virtue: standard unit headword
#113 SE
Asserting a need to preserve the __________ that became the hallmark of her predecessor’s tenure, the new director of federal monetary policy refused to subscribe to rigid or mechanistic rules in policy making.Asserting a need to preserve the __________ that became the hallmark of her predecessor’s tenure, the new director of federal monetary policy refused to subscribe to rigid or mechanistic rules in policy making.
答案：B, F　adaptability; flexibility

#114 TC SINGLE
In the solar system, collisions involving cosmic objects are among the most __________ processes shaping surfaces: images of many solar system objects show a proliferation of impact craters formed throughout the past 4.5 billion years.In the solar system, collisions involving cosmic objects are among the most __________ processes shaping surfaces: images of many solar system objects show a proliferation of impact craters formed throughout the past 4.5 billion years.
答案：B　pervasive

Blank 2

答案：A, C　abused; misused

#118 TC SINGLE
By pointing out the self-serving nature of the governor’s motives for supporting the new health care policy, the columnist implied that the governor’s idealistic-sounding explanation of her position on the issue was almost certainly __________.By pointing out the self-serving nature of the governor’s motives for supporting the new health care policy, the columnist implied that the governor’s idealistic-sounding explanation of her position on the issue was almost certainly __________.
答案：D　disingenuous

governor: standard unit headword
#119 TC DOUBLE
The claim that large budget deficits significantly depress private investment and thereby hurt future wages and living standard is (i)__________: the (ii)__________ effects of budget deficits are tiny.The claim that large budget deficits significantly depress private investment and thereby hurt future wages and living standard is (i)__________: the (ii)__________ effects of budget deficits are tiny.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　fallacious; adverse
adverse
adverse: standard unit headword
#120 TC TRIPLE
For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)__________: the witness to that is the (ii)__________ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)__________, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)__________: the witness to that is the (ii)__________ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)__________, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill

manuscript: standard unit headword

Any notion of justice in the fortunes of artists is __________: works of equal value and quality produce quite different returns or no returns at all.Any notion of justice in the fortunes of artists is __________: works of equal value and quality produce quite different returns or no returns at all.
答案：A, C　baseless; untenable

答案：E　unproven

#124 TC TRIPLE
Filler claims that after the social welfare programs of the 1960s, belief that the government has an obligation to provide decent housing for citizens who cannot afford it was (i)__________ in the United States by the notion that providing suitable shelter for everyone should be (ii)__________. Thus today in the (iii)__________ of taxpayer-sponsored initiatives, we have volunteer home-construction programs, honorable in intent but pitifully limited in scope.Filler claims that after the social welfare programs of the 1960s, belief that the government has an obligation to provide decent housing for citizens who cannot afford it was (i)__________ in the United States by the notion that providing suitable shelter for everyone should be (ii)__________. Thus today in the (iii)__________ of taxpayer-sponsored initiatives, we have volunteer home-construction programs, honorable in intent but pitifully limited in scope.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　supplanted; an act of private charity; absence
notion
notion: standard unit headword
#125 SE
One of the peculiarities of humans is that we irrationally gravitate to the predictable and avoid risk; whatever the reasons for this __________, it is hardly a sound basis for dealing with complex, long-term problems.One of the peculiarities of humans is that we irrationally gravitate to the predictable and avoid risk; whatever the reasons for this __________, it is hardly a sound basis for dealing with complex, long-term problems.
答案：B, D　predilection; proclivity

A (i) __________ of this method is that its predictions (ii) __________ actual observation, but the method also makes the questionable assumption that the future will resemble the present.One way to predict the effects of global climate change on an ecosystem is to extrapolate current trend in global change factors into the future. A (i) __________ of this method is that its predictions (ii) __________ actual observation, but the method also makes the questionable assumption that the future will resemble the present.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　virtue; derive from

virtue: standard unit headword

答案：E　topple

#128 TC TRIPLE
Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)__________. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)__________. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　distinctive; impresses considerably more than; sustain
appeal
appeal: standard unit headword
#129 SE
The author takes issue with the ideological blinders that have distorted much migration research, especially __________ modernization theorists and others for their untested assumptions of an immobile preindustrial past.The author takes issue with the ideological blinders that have distorted much migration research, especially __________ modernization theorists and others for their untested assumptions of an immobile preindustrial past.
答案：C, D　castigating; chastising

Interest in creating handheld computers is fueled by the desire to shrink the size of the electronic circuitry and to create exceptionally small mechanical systems. At this scale, however, physical (i)__________ poses unique challenges. Machining, positioning, and assembling parts by hand are easy at microscopic scales but at minute scales they are far from (ii)__________.Interest in creating handheld computers is fueled by the desire to shrink the size of the electronic circuitry and to create exceptionally small mechanical systems. At this scale, however, physical (i)__________ poses unique challenges. Machining, positioning, and assembling parts by hand are easy at microscopic scales but at minute scales they are far from (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　manipulation; routine
shrink
shrink: standard unit headword
#131 TC SINGLE
One view of historicism holds that systems of belief prevalent during different period in history are __________ and therefore cannot be understood except in their own term.One view of historicism holds that systems of belief prevalent during different period in history are __________ and therefore cannot be understood except in their own term.
答案：B　incommensurable
term
term: standard unit headword
#132 TC TRIPLE
It is possible for humans to go 40 or more hours without sleep and still be able to (i)__________ information acquired at the beginning of the sleepless period. Thus, when we are considering a role for sleep in human memory consolidation, we are referring to a possible role in the (ii)__________ encoding of information and optimizing of recall, not a (iii)__________ of sleep for recalling events of the prior day.It is possible for humans to go 40 or more hours without sleep and still be able to (i)__________ information acquired at the beginning of the sleepless period. Thus, when we are considering a role for sleep in human memory consolidation, we are referring to a possible role in the (ii)__________ encoding of information and optimizing of recall, not a (iii)__________ of sleep for recalling events of the prior day.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　disgorge; longer-term; requirement
term
term: standard unit headword
#133 SE
Despite their cultural and social significance, rapid growth, and widespread appeal in China, video games—unlike traditional media—have received __________ attention from international communication researches.Despite their cultural and social significance, rapid growth, and widespread appeal in China, video games—unlike traditional media—have received __________ attention from international communication researches.
答案：B, E　scant; limited
appeal
appeal: standard unit headword
#134 TC TRIPLE
There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. But it’s (i)__________ to (ii)__________ all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it’s just 80 or so—3 percent—that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii)__________ kinds of mosquitoes, there’s even one we’d like to see in greater numbers: Taxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. But it’s (i)__________ to (ii)__________ all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it’s just 80 or so—3 percent—that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii)__________ kinds of mosquitoes, there’s even one we’d like to see in greater numbers: Taxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　unfair; malign; blameless
rehabilitate
rehabilitate: standard unit headword

The notion that prehistoric farming of Micronesia’s Mariana Islands degraded the soils upon which savannas now occur is __________ by soil scientists, who attribute nutrient-poor savanna soils instead to long geological periods of tropical weathering.The notion that prehistoric farming of Micronesia’s Mariana Islands degraded the soils upon which savannas now occur is __________ by soil scientists, who attribute nutrient-poor savanna soils instead to long geological periods of tropical weathering.
答案：C　contradicted

Many scholars have argued that the United States Supreme Court usually (i) __________ public opinion in its decisions because it fears that it will (ii) __________ if it does not; when it does depart from public opinion, it whips up political maelstroms.Many scholars have argued that the United States Supreme Court usually (i) __________ public opinion in its decisions because it fears that it will (ii) __________ if it does not; when it does depart from public opinion, it whips up political maelstroms.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　hews to; lose public support

答案：C, E　obscure; opaque

答案：C　anomalous

Doubts and worries beset technical and scientific specialists, as well as the public at large. I do not consider such worries (ii) __________, though they are often based on intuitive feeling rather than on strictly logical arguments.
Blank 1
Blank 2

Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from

#141 SE
There are many insights in the essays collected in Observations on Modernity, but they are embedded in a dense English translation of a dense German original that may make many of them __________ to most readers.There are many insights in the essays collected in Observations on Modernity, but they are embedded in a dense English translation of a dense German original that may make many of them __________ to most readers.
答案：B, F　inaccessible; opaque

dense: standard unit headword
#142 TC TRIPLE
Industry-sponsored scientific research on chemical safety often (i)__________. Media reports regularly imply that industry support of scientific work is alone sufficient to (ii)__________ that research. Even though the source of funding has been determined to be a less significant cause of bias than other factors, industry support suffices, in the minds of many people, to (iii)__________ the credibility of scientific work.Industry-sponsored scientific research on chemical safety often (i)__________. Media reports regularly imply that industry support of scientific work is alone sufficient to (ii)__________ that research. Even though the source of funding has been determined to be a less significant cause of bias than other factors, industry support suffices, in the minds of many people, to (iii)__________ the credibility of scientific work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　elicits skepticism; invalidate; vitiate
vitiate
vitiate: standard unit headword
#143 TC SINGLE
Since the deficit predicament is fundamentally a long-term problem, the legislature’s __________ short-term approaches has actually compounded the difficulty in each succeeding year, eroding the state’s credit rating in the process.Since the deficit predicament is fundamentally a long-term problem, the legislature’s __________ short-term approaches has actually compounded the difficulty in each succeeding year, eroding the state’s credit rating in the process.
答案：B　addiction to

Blank 2
答案：B, E　irresistible; systemic

The Middle East still contains vast oil supplies. Furthermore, even if new oil finds elsewhere have been (ii) __________ than in the past, substantial quantities of oil can be profitably stripped from tar and shale.

#148 TC TRIPLE
To label Hamilton a foreigner because he was born outside what later became the United States is to assume anachronistically the existence of the nation before the fact. It is true that Hamilton came to believe that he was a (i)__________ the United States, but his (ii)__________ was stemmed not from his (iii)__________ but from his confrontation with American democracy, which he considered to be a disease afflicting the nation.To label Hamilton a foreigner because he was born outside what later became the United States is to assume anachronistically the existence of the nation before the fact. It is true that Hamilton came to believe that he was a (i)__________ the United States, but his (ii)__________ was stemmed not from his (iii)__________ but from his confrontation with American democracy, which he considered to be a disease afflicting the nation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　stranger in; alienation; birthplace
stem
stem: standard unit headword
#149 SE
Despite the __________ of medical information available through e-mail, the Internet, and mobile devices, not many patients are taking advantage of the potential of electronic communications for health-related needs.Despite the __________ of medical information available through e-mail, the Internet, and mobile devices, not many patients are taking advantage of the potential of electronic communications for health-related needs.
答案：A, D　wealth; profusion
profusion

#150 TC DOUBLE
Memoirs are inherently (i)__________, but Larry McMurtry’s volume of reminiscences about his life with books—not as a novelist but as a reader and bookstore owner—is especially (ii)__________: nearly every page sounds a note of farewell to an age of books that he sees as passing.Memoirs are inherently (i)__________, but Larry McMurtry’s volume of reminiscences about his life with books—not as a novelist but as a reader and bookstore owner—is especially (ii)__________: nearly every page sounds a note of farewell to an age of books that he sees as passing.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　wistful; valedictory
volume
volume: standard unit headword
#151 TC SINGLE
The automation of many of the functions performed at the factory, although initially inspiring __________ in many of the company’s employees, has had none of the deleterious effects forecast either within or beyond the organization.The automation of many of the functions performed at the factory, although initially inspiring __________ in many of the company’s employees, has had none of the deleterious effects forecast either within or beyond the organization.
答案：E　trepidation
deleterious
deleterious: standard unit headword
#152 TC TRIPLE
People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)__________, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)__________ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)__________ divulging the intimate details of their lives.People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)__________, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)__________ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)__________ divulging the intimate details of their lives.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　gloat; circumspect; candid in
circumspect
circumspect: standard unit headword
#153 SE
In ways large and small, millions of people are taking active steps to __________ the medical mainstream, whether by taking herbal remedies for disease or by placing their hopes for a cure in alternative treatment.In ways large and small, millions of people are taking active steps to __________ the medical mainstream, whether by taking herbal remedies for disease or by placing their hopes for a cure in alternative treatment.
答案：B, E　sidestep; circumvent
alternative
alternative: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　limitation; objectivity
volume

The experimental theater company's members know that their performances (i)__________ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)__________ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics' derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)__________ the company's work.The experimental theater company's members know that their performances (i)__________ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)__________ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics' derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)__________ the company's work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　made demands on; alien to; misunderstood

dense: standard unit headword

答案：B　adverse repercussions

答案：B, C　circuitous; indirect

Blank 2
答案：B, F　decisive; vacillation

#159 TC TRIPLE
At a recent conference on ancient DNA, one presentation opened with the claim that the field was now mature and could move ahead with confidence. This (i)__________ is (ii)__________, as demonstrated at the conference by the many presentations that notably lacked an adequate methodology for ensuring that DNA was uncontaminated by material that is more recent. In fact, ancient DNA research presents extreme technical difficulties, in part precisely because of the (iii)__________ of surviving DNA.At a recent conference on ancient DNA, one presentation opened with the claim that the field was now mature and could move ahead with confidence. This (i)__________ is (ii)__________, as demonstrated at the conference by the many presentations that notably lacked an adequate methodology for ensuring that DNA was uncontaminated by material that is more recent. In fact, ancient DNA research presents extreme technical difficulties, in part precisely because of the (iii)__________ of surviving DNA.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　optimism; unfounded; corrupted nature
unfounded

答案：B　an accommodating

答案：B, D　sleazy; sordid
appeal

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　fallacious; adverse
adverse

For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we've stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we've stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated
notion
notion: standard unit headword

答案：C　disingenuous

答案：B, E　scant; limited

The longer the migratory route, the more fat migrating birds must accumulate as fuel for their flight. The gain in body mass would make birds obese by human standards, and should render them unable to fly, but this gain is (i)__________ premigratory (ii)__________ of digestive organs, inactive during migration, and by the (iii)__________ of the muscles that power the flight.The longer the migratory route, the more fat migrating birds must accumulate as fuel for their flight. The gain in body mass would make birds obese by human standards, and should render them unable to fly, but this gain is (i)__________ premigratory (ii)__________ of digestive organs, inactive during migration, and by the (iii)__________ of the muscles that power the flight.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　partially offset by; shrinking; strengthening

答案：E　partiality

答案：D, E　checking; stemming

It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for Me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda's fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)__________. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　distinctive; impresses considerably more than; sustain

Attacking what he calls the "auteur myth"--the idea of the director as the single purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)__________--he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)__________ force in the development and production of a movie.The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)__________ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. 答案：B, E　exiguous; tenuous

Blank 2

#203 TC DOUBLE
Some academic criticism of popular novels has been (i) __________ in character, being based on the assumption that the wider the appeal, the more (ii) __________ the novel.Some academic criticism of popular novels has been (i) __________ in character, being based on the assumption that the wider the appeal, the more (ii) __________ the novel.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　elitist; undesirable
appeal
appeal: standard unit headword
#204 TC SINGLE
There is no natural environment on Earth more __________ than a virgin rain forest: a plethora of rain forest species have developed remarkably sophisticated strategies to ensure that their voices are heard.There is no natural environment on Earth more __________ than a virgin rain forest: a plethora of rain forest species have developed remarkably sophisticated strategies to ensure that their voices are heard.
答案：B　cacophonous
plethora
plethora: standard unit headword
#205 SE
Because movie studios, under pressure to generate international sales, have favored big-budget pictures with fantasy plots, the representation of everyday domestic life has largely been __________ other media, such as television and literature.Because movie studios, under pressure to generate international sales, have favored big-budget pictures with fantasy plots, the representation of everyday domestic life has largely been __________ other media, such as television and literature.
答案：A, B　left to; ceded to
fantasy
fantasy: standard unit headword
#206 TC TRIPLE
Among the rising generation of concert pianists, a (i)__________ approach to performance is sadly (ii)__________. Too often, young pianists have acquired such an approach from their teachers, the very people who should be pointing out that the flailing arm, the contorted brow, the bulging eye, and the balletic upper torso are (iii)__________ work in a business best devoted to doing the most with the least.Among the rising generation of concert pianists, a (i)__________ approach to performance is sadly (ii)__________. Too often, young pianists have acquired such an approach from their teachers, the very people who should be pointing out that the flailing arm, the contorted brow, the bulging eye, and the balletic upper torso are (iii)__________ work in a business best devoted to doing the most with the least.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　histrionic; pervasive; extraneous
extraneous
extraneous: standard unit headword
#207 TC DOUBLE
The guilty-pleasure of the play lies in its invitation for us to identify with its characters--attractive, articulate young women and young men (i) __________ behavior that, from the outside, may look more than a little (ii) __________: though the actions are initially repugnant, we cannot help but share the point of view of the characters committing them.The guilty-pleasure of the play lies in its invitation for us to identify with its characters--attractive, articulate young women and young men (i) __________ behavior that, from the outside, may look more than a little (ii) __________: though the actions are initially repugnant, we cannot help but share the point of view of the characters committing them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　rationalize; monstrous

答案：A　parsimony
governor
governor: standard unit headword
#209 SE
Blake’s reputation for weakness is __________: almost all who have worked with him say he is a disciplined, intellectually formidable, and very tough politician.Blake’s reputation for weakness is __________: almost all who have worked with him say he is a disciplined, intellectually formidable, and very tough politician.
答案：C, F　specious; unfounded
unfounded
unfounded: standard unit headword
#210 TC TRIPLE
Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)__________ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population declines that (ii)__________ individual factors and thus (iii)__________ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)__________ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population declines that (ii)__________ individual factors and thus (iii)__________ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook
adverse
adverse: standard unit headword
#211 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　adhere to; abandons

答案：C, D　castigating; chastising

答案：C　vulnerable

Throughout United States history there has been (iii) __________—seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i) __________, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii) __________ human spaceflight. Throughout United States history there has been (iii) __________—seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　utopia; prompting; the ideal location for one to better oneself

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　irresistible; systemic

appeal: standard unit headword
#216 SE
Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news—tales of how things went wrong—but we have only the most __________ discussion on how they might go right.Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news—tales of how things went wrong—but we have only the most __________ discussion on how they might go right.
答案：D, F　cursory; perfunctory
cursory
cursory: standard unit headword
#217 TC SINGLE
Though humanitarian emergencies are frequent features of television news, such exposure seldom __________ the public, which rather seems resigned to a sense of impotency.Though humanitarian emergencies are frequent features of television news, such exposure seldom __________ the public, which rather seems resigned to a sense of impotency.
答案：D　galvanizes
assuage
assuage: standard unit headword
#218 TC TRIPLE
Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)__________. The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)__________ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)__________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)__________. The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)__________ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)__________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　amenable to refutation; upended; persistent
notion
notion: standard unit headword
#219 TC DOUBLE
People tend to think of science as a gradual, steady accumulation of facts that has been ongoing for centuries. So it is often imagined that today scientists are (i) __________ an established mountain of knowledge. That idea is (ii) __________: the vast majority of nature's secrets have yet to be revealed.People tend to think of science as a gradual, steady accumulation of facts that has been ongoing for centuries. So it is often imagined that today scientists are (i) __________ an established mountain of knowledge. That idea is (ii) __________: the vast majority of nature's secrets have yet to be revealed.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　adding tiny grains to; unfounded
unfounded

答案：D　paucity

答案：C, F　burgeoning; expansion

If the data undermined positions she had previously (ii) __________, she followed the data. If the data confirmed her earlier views, she clearly stated the fact. If the data were (iii) __________, she counseled caution.Although often criticized by former allies for having abandoned or betrayed their common cause, Nguyen was actually remarkably (i) __________ over the years. Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　consistent; espoused; insufficient

Samantha Romney, however, argues that while such stories may be (ii) __________, some turtles do indeed exhibit a phenomenon known as "negligible (iii) __________," showing no signs of aging even as they pass the two-century mark.People accustomed to thinking that the human lifespan (i) __________ the outer bounds of animal longevity tend to dismiss tales of musket balls being found in the shells of living turtles. Samantha Romney, however, argues that while such stories may be (ii) __________, some turtles do indeed exhibit a phenomenon known as "negligible (iii) __________," showing no signs of aging even as they pass the two-century mark.

Blank 2

答案：B, D, I　demarcates; apocryphal; senescence

The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)__________, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to use forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)__________: the witness to that is the (ii)__________ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)__________, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to use forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill

答案：D　immutable

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　irrelevant; compelling; tainted

That assumption can lead to gross errors, as with the critical interpretations of Theodor Adorno, which are (ii) __________ by their dependence on the assumption.It is an old vice of criticism--and lately of scholarship--to assume that the meaning of artworks is (i) __________ by their creators and is then simply "there," waiting only to be decoded by a specially gifted interpreter. That assumption can lead to gross errors, as with the critical interpretations of Theodor Adorno, which are (ii) __________ by their dependence on the assumption.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　fully vested in them; vitiated

答案：A, D　inimical to; adverse to

Yet the traditional commitment to (ii)__________ was not simply (iii)__________: this new genre was expected to capture the essence of historical truth even as it allowed space for the writer's imagination.

Blank 2

答案：B, D, G　factuality; veracity; jettisoned

espouse: standard unit headword
#257 TC SINGLE
The museum exhibit perplexingly mixed significant cultural artifacts with gaudy trinkets, leading one critic to suggest that the exhibit's curator could not distinguish the important pieces from those that were __________.The museum exhibit perplexingly mixed significant cultural artifacts with gaudy trinkets, leading one critic to suggest that the exhibit's curator could not distinguish the important pieces from those that were __________.
答案：E　meretricious
illustrious
illustrious: standard unit headword
#258 TC TRIPLE
The nature of our recollection is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollection is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine
coax
coax: standard unit headword
#259 TC DOUBLE
In the years prior to the Civil War, Philadelphia's African American press encouraged readers to be vaccinated against smallpox. This journalistic campaign was initially (i) __________, appealing to readers' sense of communal duty, and became even more (ii) __________ once the war started, as smallpox outbreaks began to occur on Philadelphia's outskirts.In the years prior to the Civil War, Philadelphia's African American press encouraged readers to be vaccinated against smallpox. This journalistic campaign was initially (i) __________, appealing to readers' sense of communal duty, and became even more (ii) __________ once the war started, as smallpox outbreaks began to occur on Philadelphia's outskirts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　hortatory; urgent
hortatoryappeal

#260 TC SINGLE
Research indicates that the habitat __________ commonly observed for meadow voles and prairie voles living in a single ecosystem results primarily from species differences in habitat tolerance, with interspecific competition playing a lesser role.Research indicates that the habitat __________ commonly observed for meadow voles and prairie voles living in a single ecosystem results primarily from species differences in habitat tolerance, with interspecific competition playing a lesser role.
答案：E　segregation
alteration
alteration: standard unit headword

What they see in Jimenez is the one candidate capable of decisive leadership, in stark contrast to Diza, whose term in office has been marred by __________.What they see in Jimenez is the one candidate capable of decisive leadership, in stark contrast to Diza, whose term in office has been marred by __________.
答案：C, D　irresolution; vacillation

term: standard unit headword
#262 TC TRIPLE
Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　interchangeable; loquacious; unkempt

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　emotionally satisfying; affective

答案：C　inscrutable

答案：C, E　distinct; idiosyncratic

The present always uses the past for its own purposes, but the best historical writing attempts to (ii) __________ that inescapable impulse in order to understand history in its own terms.
Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：E　propitious

答案：B, E　transitory; ephemeral

答案：B　risk

But it's (i)__________ to (ii)__________ all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it's just 80 or so - 3 percent - that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii)__________ kinds of mosquitoes, there's even one we'd like to see in greater numbers: Toxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. However, for the opposing class, which consists of (ii) __________ systems like the global economy, human physiology, or the human mind itself, explanations of singular events are usually (iii) __________, absent the requisite accounting for the intricate dynamics of such systems.(i) __________, we can safely infer causality by appealing to a set of general principles (Newtonian mechanics, for example) that are well understood and have been shown to apply in comparably straightforward circumstances. However, for the opposing class, which consists of (ii) __________ systems like the global economy, human physiology, or the human mind itself, explanations of singular events are usually (iii) __________, absent the requisite accounting for the intricate dynamics of such systems.

Blank 2

Certainly a good science education can (ii) __________ habits of mind, but the (iii) __________ effect of education in nonscientific, humanistic subjects such as literature and history should not be underrated.Those who blame the inadequacies of science education for students' lack of reasoning skills perpetuate their unproductive notion of science as (i) __________, unique in its capacity to inoculate us against superstition and ignorance. Blank 2
Blank 3

Such a binary opposition is (iii)__________.Human brains are amazing organs but they are not perfect. Cognitive biases, as we know, sometimes produce errors. Instead, it continues to operate, sometimes under its own name, sometimes under other names. Instead, it continues to operate, sometimes under its own name, sometimes under other names. It was long believed that when both lions and hyenas were present at a kill, the latter invariably stole from the former, but this notion proved to be (ii) __________: we now know that the lion is just as (iii) __________ the hard-won prey of the hyena.The unattractive hyena is unjustly (i) __________. It was long believed that when both lions and hyenas were present at a kill, the latter invariably stole from the former, but this notion proved to be (ii) __________: we now know that the lion is just as (iii) __________ the hard-won prey of the hyena.

Blank 2
Blank 3

#301 TC TRIPLE

Current research, however, suggests that recalling a memory causes it to revert temporarily to an insecure state in which the recollection can be (ii) __________. Thus, memory is much more (iii) __________ than scientists had previously thought it to be.For decades scientists believed in the (i) __________ of long-term memories: they were unstable for a few hours but then became etched into the brain for good. Current research, however, suggests that recalling a memory causes it to revert temporarily to an insecure state in which the recollection can be (ii) __________. Thus, memory is much more (iii) __________ than scientists had previously thought it to be.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　immutability; modified; fluid

term: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　cautious; circumspect
circumspect

#303 TC DOUBLE
Conservationists tend to be (i) __________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) __________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.Conservationists tend to be (i) __________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) __________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　uncomfortable with; utilitarian

Belief in the harmful effects of the drug has all the hallmarks of a viewpoint that is not (i) __________ even though the notion that the drug has negative consequences has been (ii) __________ multiple epidemiological studies over the past decade. Yet the true believers have (iii) __________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views and continuing to lean on supporting research that was retracted long ago.Belief in the harmful effects of the drug has all the hallmarks of a viewpoint that is not (i) __________ even though the notion that the drug has negative consequences has been (ii) __________ multiple epidemiological studies over the past decade. Yet the true believers have (iii) __________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views and continuing to lean on supporting research that was retracted long ago.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amenable to refutation; upended by; persisted

答案：A, D　blithe; winsome
