methodical: standard unit headword | cursory: standard unit headword
#307 TC DOUBLE
Some historians of science maintain that the conceptual gap between successive scientific paradigms is so (i) __________ that scientists adhering to the new paradigm find it quite (ii) __________ to understand what their predecessors could have been thinking.Some historians of science maintain that the conceptual gap between successive scientific paradigms is so (i) __________ that scientists adhering to the new paradigm find it quite (ii) __________ to understand what their predecessors could have been thinking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　great; impossible
predecessor
predecessor: standard unit headword
#308 TC TRIPLE
William Newman and Lawrence Principe point out that prior to the seventeenth century the terms "alchemy" and "chemistry" were largely (i)__________ in European usage. It was from the third decade of the eighteenth century that the two terms began to (ii)__________. Newman and Principe suggest that a third term, "chymistry," should be used to denote the transitional early modern phase when "alchemy" and "chemistry" were becoming (iii)__________.William Newman and Lawrence Principe point out that prior to the seventeenth century the terms "alchemy" and "chemistry" were largely (i)__________ in European usage. It was from the third decade of the eighteenth century that the two terms began to (ii)__________. Newman and Principe suggest that a third term, "chymistry," should be used to denote the transitional early modern phase when "alchemy" and "chemistry" were becoming (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　interchangeable; diverge; steadily less synonymous
term
term: standard unit headword
#309 SE
In Ramachandran’s opinion, it is perfectly acceptable to propose bold speculations about the brain, even if these speculations seem __________; as Ramachandran frequently remarks, science thrives on risky conjecture.In Ramachandran’s opinion, it is perfectly acceptable to propose bold speculations about the brain, even if these speculations seem __________; as Ramachandran frequently remarks, science thrives on risky conjecture.
答案：A, F　unfounded; baseless
prematureunfounded
premature: standard unit headword | unfounded: standard unit headword
#310 TC DOUBLE
Audiences for daytime television soap operas have declined by 83 percent since 1991. This is partly because televised courtroom dramas, which compete with soap operas, bring to the screen some of the same elements of family (i) __________ that appeal to soap fans. And courtroom dramas have the added (ii) __________ that the domestic arguments they depict are for real.Audiences for daytime television soap operas have declined by 83 percent since 1991. This is partly because televised courtroom dramas, which compete with soap operas, bring to the screen some of the same elements of family (i) __________ that appeal to soap fans. And courtroom dramas have the added (ii) __________ that the domestic arguments they depict are for real.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　wrangling; frisson
appeal
appeal: standard unit headword
#311 TC TRIPLE
Although Sapir and Whorf's notion that different languages may impart different cognitive skills met with (i) __________ early on, they had little evidence to support their claim. By the 1970s many scientists had become (ii) __________ their hypothesis, and it was largely (iii) __________ as a new set of theories claiming that language and thought are universal came to dominate the field.Although Sapir and Whorf's notion that different languages may impart different cognitive skills met with (i) __________ early on, they had little evidence to support their claim. By the 1970s many scientists had become (ii) __________ their hypothesis, and it was largely (iii) __________ as a new set of theories claiming that language and thought are universal came to dominate the field.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　excitement; disenchanted with; abandoned
notion
notion: standard unit headword

In order to keep the museum's admission lines moving, security inspections are mostly quite __________.In order to keep the museum's admission lines moving, security inspections are mostly quite __________.
答案：C, D　cursory; casual

#313 TC DOUBLE
Because the requerimiento, a formal statement in Spanish that was read to indigenous people in Southwestern pueblos during the Coronado expedition in the 1540s, was replete with concepts and terms that (i) __________ the pueblo world of that day, its audience likely found it (ii) __________.Because the requerimiento, a formal statement in Spanish that was read to indigenous people in Southwestern pueblos during the Coronado expedition in the 1540s, was replete with concepts and terms that (i) __________ the pueblo world of that day, its audience likely found it (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　lacked equivalents in; inscrutable

term: standard unit headword
#314 TC TRIPLE
Materials expand and contract with temperature changes. This general concept has been exploited for thousands of years, for instance, to ensure tight rivets in blacksmithing by shrink-fitting. For precision instruments, however, (i)__________ materials are truly (ii)__________: hence the importance of Invar, an alloy notable for its (iii)__________ expansion and contraction over a range of temperatures.Materials expand and contract with temperature changes. This general concept has been exploited for thousands of years, for instance, to ensure tight rivets in blacksmithing by shrink-fitting. For precision instruments, however, (i)__________ materials are truly (ii)__________: hence the importance of Invar, an alloy notable for its (iii)__________ expansion and contraction over a range of temperatures.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　dimensionally stable; paramount; resistance to
shrink
shrink: standard unit headword
#315 TC DOUBLE
Because self-defense was peripheral to the karate training techniques instituted throughout the Okinawan educational system in the early twentieth century, the pugilistic dimension of karate techniques was (i) __________ or even (ii) __________.Because self-defense was peripheral to the karate training techniques instituted throughout the Okinawan educational system in the early twentieth century, the pugilistic dimension of karate techniques was (i) __________ or even (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　intentionally downplayed; eliminated
dimension

#316 TC TRIPLE
Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey's book is representative of a heightened sense of (i) __________. Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii) __________ the (iii) __________ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey's book is representative of a heightened sense of (i) __________. Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii) __________ the (iii) __________ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　candor; antidote to; banal notion

Although the virtues of supplementing wild fish populations with fish from hatcheries are often __________, scientific studies have indicated that fish stocking can harm the wild, native species of the water body.Although the virtues of supplementing wild fish populations with fish from hatcheries are often __________, scientific studies have indicated that fish stocking can harm the wild, native species of the water body.
答案：B, E　extolled; acclaimed

Blank 2
答案：C, D　obfuscate; garner an artificial

#319 TC TRIPLE
The only way to definitively test a large civil engineering structure such as a bridge is to build it in anticipation of possible challenges from nature and then let nature take its course. That is not to say that every new large structure is a (i) __________. Engineers understand a great deal about the behavior and limitations of their structures' components. Furthermore, it is the (ii) __________ structure that is built more than 10 or 20 percent larger than its predecessors, thus allowing engineers to move (iii) __________ into unknown territory.The only way to definitively test a large civil engineering structure such as a bridge is to build it in anticipation of possible challenges from nature and then let nature take its course. That is not to say that every new large structure is a (i) __________. Engineers understand a great deal about the behavior and limitations of their structures' components. Furthermore, it is the (ii) __________ structure that is built more than 10 or 20 percent larger than its predecessors, thus allowing engineers to move (iii) __________ into unknown territory.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　gamble; unusual; incrementally
predecessor
predecessor: standard unit headword
#320 SE
Because they view Earth's resources as strictly limited, some environmentalists find the notion of sustainable growth to be __________ believing that self-restraint and sacrifice must be at the heart of any environmentally sound strategy for the future.Because they view Earth's resources as strictly limited, some environmentalists find the notion of sustainable growth to be __________ believing that self-restraint and sacrifice must be at the heart of any environmentally sound strategy for the future.
答案：C, D　dubious; questionable

Blank 2

答案：C, F　undermine; contradict

The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) __________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) __________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) __________.The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) __________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) __________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　prosper; benefits from; perpetuated

答案：E, F　convoluted; intricate

Singer was a prominent and beloved figure on the American literary scene for so long that it is hard to imagine that the flurry of publications marking the centennial of his birth would have anything __________ to say about him.I.B. Singer was a prominent and beloved figure on the American literary scene for so long that it is hard to imagine that the flurry of publications marking the centennial of his birth would have anything __________ to say about him.

#331 TC TRIPLE
The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　difficult; absurd; explained

答案：B, C　incomplete; deficient

Blank 2

However, there is little empirical evidence for a relationship between invasion and extinction, and it is not (ii) __________ for native and nonnative species to thrive after the nonnative species' introduction, because the habitat's conditions favor both. When native species do decline after nonnative species are introduced, the reason may be a human-caused habitat alteration that affects native and nonnative species (iii) __________.When the introduction of a nonnative species into a habitat correlates with the decline of a species native to that habitat, observers may (i) __________ a causal relationship. However, there is little empirical evidence for a relationship between invasion and extinction, and it is not (ii) __________ for native and nonnative species to thrive after the nonnative species' introduction, because the habitat's conditions favor both. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　infer; unusual; differentially

答案：A, D　attraction; benefit

答案：B, E, H　complex; connection to; more direct

答案：A, D　fade; wane

Blank 2

But she takes it down only after it has had its entirely (iii) __________ flutter in the breeze.

Blank 2
Blank 3

complacent: standard unit headword
#355 TC TRIPLE
Ironically, contemporary critics who celebrate the Victorian novel for its intellectual depth describe the (i) __________ effects of newer media - film, television, digital technology - in precisely the same terms that earlier critics had used to (ii) __________ the novel itself: as piecemeal entertainments that (iii) __________ mental effort on the part of the consumer.Ironically, contemporary critics who celebrate the Victorian novel for its intellectual depth describe the (i) __________ effects of newer media - film, television, digital technology - in precisely the same terms that earlier critics had used to (ii) __________ the novel itself: as piecemeal entertainments that (iii) __________ mental effort on the part of the consumer.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　numbing; lambast; obviate
term
term: standard unit headword
#356 SE
Sand dunes are __________ because the fine sand particles on the surface of the dune are poorly bonded and susceptible to erosion by wind and rain.Sand dunes are __________ because the fine sand particles on the surface of the dune are poorly bonded and susceptible to erosion by wind and rain.
答案：D, F　transient; unstable
fine
fine: standard unit headword
#357 TC DOUBLE
From time to time, all scientific disciplines encounter observations that do not fit the theories of the day. At first such observations tend to be treated as (i) __________, but those scientists (ii) __________ them sometimes discover that they have to abandon established principles in order to account for the new findings.From time to time, all scientific disciplines encounter observations that do not fit the theories of the day. At first such observations tend to be treated as (i) __________, but those scientists (ii) __________ them sometimes discover that they have to abandon established principles in order to account for the new findings.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　anomalies; willing to investigate
harbinger
harbinger: standard unit headword
#358 TC TRIPLE
Biographies tumble off the presses in profusion these days, a (i) __________ linked, for better or worse, to the current obsession with celebrity. Some critics complain that biographers too often (ii) __________ the messy reality of a life, and it's true that lesser authors simply glorify or, more often, vilify their subjects. But good biographers are far more respectful of (iii) __________: the best see not one monolithic truth about a person but many overlapping truths.Biographies tumble off the presses in profusion these days, a (i) __________ linked, for better or worse, to the current obsession with celebrity. Some critics complain that biographers too often (ii) __________ the messy reality of a life, and it's true that lesser authors simply glorify or, more often, vilify their subjects. But good biographers are far more respectful of (iii) __________: the best see not one monolithic truth about a person but many overlapping truths.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　boom; impose a specious coherence on; complexity
profusion
profusion: standard unit headword
#359 SE
Cunliffe is intellectually indebted to the Annales school of French economic and social historians, which emphasized largely static environmental influences and long-term historical continuity and regarded political events mainly as __________.Cunliffe is intellectually indebted to the Annales school of French economic and social historians, which emphasized largely static environmental influences and long-term historical continuity and regarded political events mainly as __________.
答案：D, F　trivia; ephemera
term
term: standard unit headword
#360 TC DOUBLE
A hallmark of certain nineteenth-century mystery novels was the reform agenda of their authors, who ostensibly sought to expose economic injustice while depicting the seamy underside of urban life. In reality, however, these claims to a radical political agenda were often (i) __________ meant to give lurid thrillers the appearance of (ii) __________.A hallmark of certain nineteenth-century mystery novels was the reform agenda of their authors, who ostensibly sought to expose economic injustice while depicting the seamy underside of urban life. In reality, however, these claims to a radical political agenda were often (i) __________ meant to give lurid thrillers the appearance of (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　disingenuous; high-minded purpose
appeal
appeal: standard unit headword
#361 TC TRIPLE
The notion of popular science or of the public understanding of science is very much a concern in today's world, where we can see (i) __________ between science and nonscience. But in the nineteenth century, there was still an enviable mixing and cross-fertilization between seemingly (ii) __________ subjects, and there was no (iii) __________ what counted as science, let alone how a public understanding might differ from any other sort of understanding.The notion of popular science or of the public understanding of science is very much a concern in today's world, where we can see (i) __________ between science and nonscience. But in the nineteenth century, there was still an enviable mixing and cross-fertilization between seemingly (ii) __________ subjects, and there was no (iii) __________ what counted as science, let alone how a public understanding might differ from any other sort of understanding.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　a clear demarcation; disparate; consensus on
notion
notion: standard unit headword
#362 SE
Fedigan argues that, in actuality, ethologists who claim to __________ anthropomorphism often end up simply substituting one set of anthropomorphic terms for another.Fedigan argues that, in actuality, ethologists who claim to __________ anthropomorphism often end up simply substituting one set of anthropomorphic terms for another.
答案：A, F　eschew; avoid
term
term: standard unit headword
#363 TC DOUBLE
In the popular conception, (i) __________ is inextricably tied up with (ii) __________: doing something truly creative, we are inclined to think, requires the freshness and energy of youth. Orson Welles made his masterpiece, Citizen Kane, at twenty-five, and Mozart wrote his breakthrough Piano concerto no.9 at twenty-one.In the popular conception, (i) __________ is inextricably tied up with (ii) __________: doing something truly creative, we are inclined to think, requires the freshness and energy of youth. Orson Welles made his masterpiece, Citizen Kane, at twenty-five, and Mozart wrote his breakthrough Piano concerto no.9 at twenty-one.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　genius; precocity
dedication
dedication: standard unit headword
#364 TC TRIPLE
Literary fiction is (i) __________ genre fiction. Why? Because its plots are less predictable, its characters deeper, its reality (ii) __________ actual reality. That, at least, is the commonly held view, shared even by readers, and writers, of genre fiction--or so it appears from their readiness to (iii) __________ their tastes. But many new "serious" novels are just as constrained by their genre as any multivolume fantasy epic or cheap romance.Literary fiction is (i) __________ genre fiction. Why? Because its plots are less predictable, its characters deeper, its reality (ii) __________ actual reality. That, at least, is the commonly held view, shared even by readers, and writers, of genre fiction--or so it appears from their readiness to (iii) __________ their tastes. But many new "serious" novels are just as constrained by their genre as any multivolume fantasy epic or cheap romance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　superior to; closer to; apologize for

fantasy: standard unit headword
#365 SE
Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially __________ nature.Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially __________ nature.
答案：A, F　pugnacious; truculent

complacent: standard unit headword
#366 TC DOUBLE
Research notes that wolves' otherwise strongly hierarchical society is marked by occasional displays of populist (i) __________: if a pack leader proves a too-snappish tyrant, subordinate wolves will (ii) __________ the top cur.Research notes that wolves' otherwise strongly hierarchical society is marked by occasional displays of populist (i) __________: if a pack leader proves a too-snappish tyrant, subordinate wolves will (ii) __________ the top cur.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　umbrage; collectively overthrow

#367 TC TRIPLE
Unlike some of his predecessors and competitors, Schaeffer's distinguished position as a foreign-affairs columnist has not made him (i) __________. His column is breezy and conversational. In light-handed prose he reports what he sees with his own eyes and what he learns from his trips around the globe. To further (ii) __________ an already (iii) __________ style, he shares with his readers occasional heartwarming references to his family.Unlike some of his predecessors and competitors, Schaeffer's distinguished position as a foreign-affairs columnist has not made him (i) __________. His column is breezy and conversational. In light-handed prose he reports what he sees with his own eyes and what he learns from his trips around the globe. To further (ii) __________ an already (iii) __________ style, he shares with his readers occasional heartwarming references to his family.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　pontifical; enliven; familiar
predecessor
predecessor: standard unit headword
#368 SE
Although one can adduce myriad of examples of ecosystem disruption by nonindigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survived in fact appear to have quite __________ effects on the ecosystem they have invaded.Although one can adduce myriad of examples of ecosystem disruption by nonindigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survived in fact appear to have quite __________ effects on the ecosystem they have invaded.
答案：C, E　minimal; trifling

This cautious approach makes diplomatic sense, since attempts over the past 15 years to reach international agreements on patents have (ii) __________ just such sensitive issues.
Blank 1
Blank 2

#372 TC DOUBLE
Journalist Michael Pollan is nothing if not (i) __________, committed to investigating our eating habits through (ii) __________ and unwilling to pass judgment on any food-related practice that he has not witnessed firsthand or even joined in.Journalist Michael Pollan is nothing if not (i) __________, committed to investigating our eating habits through (ii) __________ and unwilling to pass judgment on any food-related practice that he has not witnessed firsthand or even joined in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　an empiricist; careful observation

#373 TC TRIPLE
There can be little question that by the fourteenth century the capacity to (i) __________ a doubtless restricted written Latin was available in virtually all English communities. It would, of course, be (ii) __________ to suppose that all the wealth of material available in Latin since 1100 was put to service in the cultural formation of every writer of business Latin in provincial England, but even schoolbooks (iii) __________ some elements of classical education. Latin literacy was not, in these circumstances, exclusive.There can be little question that by the fourteenth century the capacity to (i) __________ a doubtless restricted written Latin was available in virtually all English communities. It would, of course, be (ii) __________ to suppose that all the wealth of material available in Latin since 1100 was put to service in the cultural formation of every writer of business Latin in provincial England, but even schoolbooks (iii) __________ some elements of classical education. Latin literacy was not, in these circumstances, exclusive.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　deploy; fanciful; purveyed

suppose: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　persistence; survival

People are individually altruistic social animals who nonetheless teach themselves to be (ii) __________ in the public sphere.

Blank 2

The prime minister underestimates the fatigue that her continual (i)__________ induces, even among those who are largely (ii)__________ her aims. Constantly referring to the bold challenges and tremendous opportunities that she and the country face, the prime minister insists that she is (iii)__________ the country, when most people would be grateful if she succeeded just in improving it a bit.The prime minister underestimates the fatigue that her continual (i)__________ induces, even among those who are largely (ii)__________ her aims. Constantly referring to the bold challenges and tremendous opportunities that she and the country face, the prime minister insists that she is (iii)__________ the country, when most people would be grateful if she succeeded just in improving it a bit.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　overclaiming; sympathetic to; radically transforming

答案：B, E　exalt; valorize

But the (ii) __________ possibilities of photography have been present from the medium's birth. Distortions of truth not only occur mechanically (through shutter speed, for example) and in printing (as through choice of chemicals and paper) but are (iii) __________ to the very idea of photographers being stylistically different from one another.Since readily available computer programs now allow photographers to manipulate their images with the same (i) __________ as painters, photography's claim to any inherent truth has just about evaporated. But the (ii) __________ possibilities of photography have been present from the medium's birth. Distortions of truth not only occur mechanically (through shutter speed, for example) and in printing (as through choice of chemicals and paper) but are (iii) __________ to the very idea of photographers being stylistically different from one another.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　license for fiction; fictive; integral

One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predated that event

Although its gray text blocks and black-and-white illustrations give it a sober mien, this one-stop resource can take the place of a dozen less __________ texts.Although its gray text blocks and black-and-white illustrations give it a sober mien, this one-stop resource can take the place of a dozen less __________ texts.

#385 TC TRIPLE
When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine

As a result, scientists operate in a realm where the incentive structure serves as an (i) __________ greater (ii) __________ in the profession.
Blank 1
Blank 2

It is hard to know exactly what is causing a worldwide proliferation of jellyfish, but we can speculate. The fishing industry has (i) __________ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. And when small, plankton-eating fish, such as anchovies, are (ii) __________, jellyfish flourish, gorging on plankton and reproducing prolifically.It is hard to know exactly what is causing a worldwide proliferation of jellyfish, but we can speculate. The fishing industry has (i) __________ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. And when small, plankton-eating fish, such as anchovies, are (ii) __________, jellyfish flourish, gorging on plankton and reproducing prolifically.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　depleted; overharvested
safeguard
safeguard: standard unit headword
#403 SE
We do not always use words in accordance with their dictionary definitions, for meaning often fluctuates with context; that does not mean, however, that we are completely __________ in how we use language.We do not always use words in accordance with their dictionary definitions, for meaning often fluctuates with context; that does not mean, however, that we are completely __________ in how we use language.
答案：B, E　unconstrained; free

methodical: standard unit headword
#404 TC TRIPLE
Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)__________. Instead of (ii)__________ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)__________. You, not the author, are the master.Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)__________. Instead of (ii)__________ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)__________. You, not the author, are the master.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　empowering; surrendering to; glean precisely what you want from it
term
term: standard unit headword
#405 TC DOUBLE
In clinical research into new treatments or the causal factors of diseases, randomized, double-blind controlled clinical trials are seen as (i) __________ but, given their (ii) __________, often are not feasible.In clinical research into new treatments or the causal factors of diseases, randomized, double-blind controlled clinical trials are seen as (i) __________ but, given their (ii) __________, often are not feasible.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　the gold standard; expense
appeal
appeal: standard unit headword
#406 SE
While normal floods resulting from usual monsoon rainfall are __________ the grows of crops, recently there has been an increase in the frequency high intensity floods that do not have such welcome effects.While normal floods resulting from usual monsoon rainfall are __________ the grows of crops, recently there has been an increase in the frequency high intensity floods that do not have such welcome effects.
答案：E, F　essential for; indispensable to
deleterious
deleterious: standard unit headword
#407 TC TRIPLE
When the Agriculture Department (i)__________ its dietary guidelines, it laid down a challenge: Eat better, smarter, and healthier or else. The "or else" included a long list of (ii)__________ that (iii)__________ the developed world, from heart disease and osteoporosis to diabetes.When the Agriculture Department (i)__________ its dietary guidelines, it laid down a challenge: Eat better, smarter, and healthier or else. The "or else" included a long list of (ii)__________ that (iii)__________ the developed world, from heart disease and osteoporosis to diabetes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　make public; maladies; plague
assuage
assuage: standard unit headword
#408 TC DOUBLE
Biologist Catherine Graham, studying toucans in Mexico, predicted that because toucans prefer relatively large forest patches and feed on fruit, they would fly more often to (i) __________ patches and to patches with abundant fruit resources than to small patches or those that had (ii) __________ fruit resources.Biologist Catherine Graham, studying toucans in Mexico, predicted that because toucans prefer relatively large forest patches and feed on fruit, they would fly more often to (i) __________ patches and to patches with abundant fruit resources than to small patches or those that had (ii) __________ fruit resources.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　sizable; scant
dense
dense: standard unit headword
#409 SE
The writer’s assessment of the critic includes personal __________, such as jibes about his physical girth and style of delivery, and is not the better for it.The writer’s assessment of the critic includes personal __________, such as jibes about his physical girth and style of delivery, and is not the better for it.
答案：A, F　aspersions; slurs
fantasy
fantasy: standard unit headword
#410 TC TRIPLE
One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the Western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the Western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predate that event
virtue
virtue: standard unit headword
#411 TC DOUBLE
The (i) __________ museums worldwide makes for (ii) __________: by some estimates, 75 percent of species that are "discovered" each year during field expeditions are already part of a museum collection somewhere.The (i) __________ museums worldwide makes for (ii) __________: by some estimates, 75 percent of species that are "discovered" each year during field expeditions are already part of a museum collection somewhere.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　inattention to; lost opportunities
unfounded
unfounded: standard unit headword
#412 TC TRIPLE
When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted towards positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more often considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted towards positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more often considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
articulate
articulate: standard unit headword
#413 SE
The concept of increasing complexity of organisms has __________ history among evolutionary biologists, and yet many laypeople would unhesitatingly say that the pattern applies to the history of life on Earth.The concept of increasing complexity of organisms has __________ history among evolutionary biologists, and yet many laypeople would unhesitatingly say that the pattern applies to the history of life on Earth.
答案：D, F　a contentious; a fraught
illustrious
illustrious: standard unit headword
#414 TC DOUBLE
One radical approach to literary translation is to (i) __________ creating a (ii) __________ reproduction of the original work and simply to compose a new work, but in a manner inspired by the older one.One radical approach to literary translation is to (i) __________ creating a (ii) __________ reproduction of the original work and simply to compose a new work, but in a manner inspired by the older one.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　despair of; faithful
dense
dense: standard unit headword
#415 SE
The medical researchers replied to the charge that their proposed new treatment was __________ by demonstrating that it in fact observed standard medical practices.The medical researchers replied to the charge that their proposed new treatment was __________ by demonstrating that it in fact observed standard medical practices.
答案：C, F　unorthodox; unconventional
deleterious
deleterious: standard unit headword
#416 TC TRIPLE
So (i)__________ is the reputation of the country's police for corruption and other forms of (ii)__________ that it has become a kind of tradition that every newly appointed police chief pledges to (iii)__________ the force.So (i)__________ is the reputation of the country's police for corruption and other forms of (ii)__________ that it has become a kind of tradition that every newly appointed police chief pledges to (iii)__________ the force.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　paralyzing; criminality; reform
unfounded
unfounded: standard unit headword
#417 TC DOUBLE
Dismissing the argument that the author's intent should be (i) __________ any interpretation of a literary work, the critic contended that while authorial intentions were (ii) __________ to some degree, they should not necessarily have primacy in every case.Dismissing the argument that the author's intent should be (i) __________ any interpretation of a literary work, the critic contended that while authorial intentions were (ii) __________ to some degree, they should not necessarily have primacy in every case.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　paramount in; germane
extraneous
extraneous: standard unit headword
#418 TC TRIPLE
Not all paleontologists agree that connections between the continents were (i)__________ just after the extinction of the dinosaurs. Some hold the view that North America, Asia, and South America had (ii)__________ immediately following the dinosaur extinction, pointing to (iii)__________ between ancient kinds of mammals that existed on all three continents at this time in support of their argument.Not all paleontologists agree that connections between the continents were (i)__________ just after the extinction of the dinosaurs. Some hold the view that North America, Asia, and South America had (ii)__________ immediately following the dinosaur extinction, pointing to (iii)__________ between ancient kinds of mammals that existed on all three continents at this time in support of their argument.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　limited; extensive contacts; similarities

lasting: standard unit headword

答案：A, F　aspersions; slurs

Giffuni is recognized as a (i) __________ of the medium. Her efforts (ii) __________ her hope that the art will reach a higher level of recognition.

Blank 2
答案：B, D　mainstay; reflect

#421 TC TRIPLE
Barker's account of how mores have evolved over time is illuminating. It reveals as (i)__________ and (ii)__________ some values that have often been regarded as (iii)__________, while uncovering other values that do indeed seem to be universal.Barker's account of how mores have evolved over time is illuminating. It reveals as (i)__________ and (ii)__________ some values that have often been regarded as (iii)__________, while uncovering other values that do indeed seem to be universal.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　transient; relative; nonnegotiable

Though only nine years old, the dancer possesses __________ flexibility that suggests she may become a balletic superstar.Though only nine years old, the dancer possesses __________ flexibility that suggests she may become a balletic superstar.
答案：D, E　a preternatural; a prodigious
ungainly
ungainly: standard unit headword
#423 TC DOUBLE
The humor in this play derives from its (i)__________. The new production, however, inexplicably goes in the opposite direction; it is so (ii)__________ that the audience does not even seem to realize that the play is supposed to be a comedy.The humor in this play derives from its (i)__________. The new production, however, inexplicably goes in the opposite direction; it is so (ii)__________ that the audience does not even seem to realize that the play is supposed to be a comedy.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　verbal nimbleness; plodding
allusionsupposesupposed
allusion: standard unit headword | suppose: standard unit headword | supposed: standard unit headword
#424 TC TRIPLE
Many historians of the ancient world are wary of sounding (i)__________. Write so much as a sentence and the temptation is immediately to (ii)__________ it. Even in cases when the sources for a given event are (iii)__________, uncertainties and discrepancies crop up everywhere.Many historians of the ancient world are wary of sounding (i)__________. Write so much as a sentence and the temptation is immediately to (ii)__________ it. Even in cases when the sources for a given event are (iii)__________, uncertainties and discrepancies crop up everywhere.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　dogmatic; qualify; plentiful

forswear: standard unit headword
#425 SE
Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong--and, consequently, we are lacking in any __________ discussion on how they might go right.Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong--and, consequently, we are lacking in any __________ discussion on how they might go right.
答案：A, C　incisive; trenchant
cursory
cursory: standard unit headword
#426 TC DOUBLE
The idea of traveling in order to find natural extremes and wonders was (i) __________ in eighteenth- and nineteenth-century Europe, and many European travelers of the period were drawn to Iceland and other North Atlantic countries as places where they might find these (ii) __________ landscapes.The idea of traveling in order to find natural extremes and wonders was (i) __________ in eighteenth- and nineteenth-century Europe, and many European travelers of the period were drawn to Iceland and other North Atlantic countries as places where they might find these (ii) __________ landscapes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　not uncommon; exotic
landscapearticulate
landscape: standard unit headword | articulate: standard unit headword
#427 TC TRIPLE
His new role gives the normally clownish actor a chance to impress audiences with his (i) __________. He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Perhaps without quite knowing it, the audience waits for a wink, a hint that some of the (ii) __________ spirit that animated his previous movies might be lurking inside the (iii) __________ manner he presents in this film.His new role gives the normally clownish actor a chance to impress audiences with his (i) __________. He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Perhaps without quite knowing it, the audience waits for a wink, a hint that some of the (ii) __________ spirit that animated his previous movies might be lurking inside the (iii) __________ manner he presents in this film.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　forbearance; madcap; reserved
lugubrious
lugubrious: standard unit headword
#428 SE
Although anecdotes about her abound, it is difficult to verify facts about al-Khansa'--like many early poetic figures, she may well be a composite figure, her corpus __________ the works of many women poets.Although anecdotes about her abound, it is difficult to verify facts about al-Khansa'--like many early poetic figures, she may well be a composite figure, her corpus __________ the works of many women poets.
答案：B, F　an amalgamation of; a fusion of
harbinger
harbinger: standard unit headword
#429 TC TRIPLE
At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)__________ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)__________ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)__________ in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)__________ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)__________ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)__________ in its effects on habitat.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique
transitory
transitory: standard unit headword
#430 SE
Strayed's advice columns were a clear step away from the __________ of societal norms by earlier columnists; instead, her approach was one of liberating, nonjudgmental permissiveness.Strayed's advice columns were a clear step away from the __________ of societal norms by earlier columnists; instead, her approach was one of liberating, nonjudgmental permissiveness.
答案：D, F　reinforcement; bolstering

forswear: standard unit headword
#431 TC TRIPLE
Anthony Kenny states that although his A New History of Western Philosophy is intended for undergraduates, he aimed to write it in a lighthearted manner that will give (i)__________ to those who read the history "not for curricular purposes but for their own enjoyment". His book is certainly enlightening, and although the bibliography includes technical works liable to (ii)__________ the newcomer, the work is written at a level that generally (iii)__________ between elementary and advanced.Anthony Kenny states that although his A New History of Western Philosophy is intended for undergraduates, he aimed to write it in a lighthearted manner that will give (i)__________ to those who read the history "not for curricular purposes but for their own enjoyment". His book is certainly enlightening, and although the bibliography includes technical works liable to (ii)__________ the newcomer, the work is written at a level that generally (iii)__________ between elementary and advanced.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　pleasure; baffle; strikes a good balance
fine
fine: standard unit headword
#432 SE
Some renewable energy technologies are quite __________; for example, hydro power from dams and rivers has been a source of power generation since the early 1900s, and today it supplies sixteen percent of electricity worldwide.Some renewable energy technologies are quite __________; for example, hydro power from dams and rivers has been a source of power generation since the early 1900s, and today it supplies sixteen percent of electricity worldwide.
答案：C, D　mainstream; conventional
appeal
appeal: standard unit headword
#433 TC TRIPLE
The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)__________ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)__________, for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)__________.The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)__________ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)__________, for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　spontaneity; immediacy; improvisation
compassion
compassion: standard unit headword
#434 SE
Artist Uzo Egonu's transition from a cultural agenda to a political one was less premeditated than it was __________, imposed upon him by the political turmoil, in the late 1960s, in his native Nigeria.Artist Uzo Egonu's transition from a cultural agenda to a political one was less premeditated than it was __________, imposed upon him by the political turmoil, in the late 1960s, in his native Nigeria.
答案：A, C　exigent; imperative
circumspect
circumspect: standard unit headword

Yet when problems are (ii)__________, or require combining varied sources, computers are (iii)__________ human intelligence.Give a computer (i)__________ task - winning at chess, say, or predicting the weather - and the machine bests humans nearly every time. Yet when problems are (ii)__________, or require combining varied sources, computers are (iii)__________ human intelligence.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　a well-defined; nuanced; no match for

答案：C, E　form; architecture

Blank 1

Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　appreciated; kept them at a distance; contributing to

答案：C, E　form; architecture

longevity: standard unit headword
#439 TC TRIPLE
Pine stumps have been found in discrete layers in peat deposits throughout western Europe and thus represent brief but distinct episodes of bog colonization by pine. Their occurrence implies conditions on the bog surface suitable for colonization, followed by inhospitable conditions for trees that nevertheless facilitated preservation of stumps. Thus, the (i)__________ of pine stumps can (ii)__________ climate change, by (iii)__________ the inference that bog surfaces had dried sufficiently to allow colonization and then became too wet to support the trees.Pine stumps have been found in discrete layers in peat deposits throughout western Europe and thus represent brief but distinct episodes of bog colonization by pine. Their occurrence implies conditions on the bog surface suitable for colonization, followed by inhospitable conditions for trees that nevertheless facilitated preservation of stumps. Thus, the (i)__________ of pine stumps can (ii)__________ climate change, by (iii)__________ the inference that bog surfaces had dried sufficiently to allow colonization and then became too wet to support the trees.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　presence; function as a proxy for; supporting
articulate
articulate: standard unit headword
#440 SE
The __________ of the folktales in this collection does not inhibit the conveyance of important lessons; indeed, the didactic effect would be lost if the stories were more protracted.The __________ of the folktales in this collection does not inhibit the conveyance of important lessons; indeed, the didactic effect would be lost if the stories were more protracted.
答案：D, F　brevity; economy
gaiety
gaiety: standard unit headword
#441 TC TRIPLE
Predators try to (i) __________ their ultimate energy intake by (ii) __________ the effort they expend searching for and handling prey relative to the energy the prey provides. Thus, a particular kind of prey would be (iii) __________ to a predator's diet only if it is worth the predator's time and energy--that is, if it increases the animal's overall energy profit during foraging.Predators try to (i) __________ their ultimate energy intake by (ii) __________ the effort they expend searching for and handling prey relative to the energy the prey provides. Thus, a particular kind of prey would be (iii) __________ to a predator's diet only if it is worth the predator's time and energy--that is, if it increases the animal's overall energy profit during foraging.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　maximize; reducing; added
deleterious
deleterious: standard unit headword
#442 SE
Although oceanic water currents can appear to be almost completely __________, scientists recognize the existence of invisible separators, which they call transport barriers, and these maintain a measure of order and structure in the chaotic flow.Although oceanic water currents can appear to be almost completely __________, scientists recognize the existence of invisible separators, which they call transport barriers, and these maintain a measure of order and structure in the chaotic flow.
答案：A, F　uncontrolled; random
transitory
transitory: standard unit headword
#443 TC TRIPLE
Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) __________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) __________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) __________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) __________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) __________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) __________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　subvert; ecumenical; a pastiche of styles
transitory
transitory: standard unit headword
#444 SE
Though some of the former leader's policies had been officially critiqued, the ruling party could hardly __________ him because it had built much of its legitimacy upon his charisma.Though some of the former leader's policies had been officially critiqued, the ruling party could hardly __________ him because it had built much of its legitimacy upon his charisma.
答案：C, F　renounce; disavow
espouse
espouse: standard unit headword
#445 TC TRIPLE
Certain molecular studies imply that, contrary to conventional scientific wisdom, neornithines (modern birds) lived alongside dinosaurs instead of having evolved after dinosaurs' extinction. It seems odd to imagine a duck paddling alongside a Spinosaurus, but the molecular evidence for the (i) __________ of modern birds and dinosaurs is so (ii) __________ that even paleontologists--who have typically viewed with skepticism those DNA findings that conflict with the fossil record--have begun to (iii) __________ it.Certain molecular studies imply that, contrary to conventional scientific wisdom, neornithines (modern birds) lived alongside dinosaurs instead of having evolved after dinosaurs' extinction. It seems odd to imagine a duck paddling alongside a Spinosaurus, but the molecular evidence for the (i) __________ of modern birds and dinosaurs is so (ii) __________ that even paleontologists--who have typically viewed with skepticism those DNA findings that conflict with the fossil record--have begun to (iii) __________ it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　contemporaneity; compelling; embrace
longevity
longevity: standard unit headword
#446 SE
Lena's predisposition toward __________ is reflected in her use of possessions for as long as she can before replacing them.Lena's predisposition toward __________ is reflected in her use of possessions for as long as she can before replacing them.
答案：A, C　parsimony; economy
profusion
profusion: standard unit headword
#447 TC TRIPLE
According to Merrin, many law school curricula are (i) __________ the challenge of fostering an adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with, core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a (ii) __________ ethics, thus creating an (iii) __________ audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.According to Merrin, many law school curricula are (i) __________ the challenge of fostering an adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with, core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a (ii) __________ ethics, thus creating an (iii) __________ audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　unequal to; cynical sense of; unfavorable
notion
notion: standard unit headword
#448 SE
Heidegger's philosophy of science tends to give prominence to visuals and visualization because for Heidegger, truth-including scientific truth-is the __________ of unconcealment: it is the lifting of a veil.Heidegger's philosophy of science tends to give prominence to visuals and visualization because for Heidegger, truth-including scientific truth-is the __________ of unconcealment: it is the lifting of a veil.
答案：A, F　consequence; result
predecessor
predecessor: standard unit headword
#449 TC TRIPLE
Neolithic people living in an arid northern region of China cultivated common millet more than did people living to their south. Common millet is easy to cultivate and appears to have been better suited to primitive agricultural practices than were other crops. Under (i) __________ environmental conditions, however, common millet cultivation may be (ii) __________ because of the millet's relatively (iii) __________ response to high soil fertility and abundant water availability.Neolithic people living in an arid northern region of China cultivated common millet more than did people living to their south. Common millet is easy to cultivate and appears to have been better suited to primitive agricultural practices than were other crops. Under (i) __________ environmental conditions, however, common millet cultivation may be (ii) __________ because of the millet's relatively (iii) __________ response to high soil fertility and abundant water availability.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　propitious; a disadvantageous choice; limited
adverse
adverse: standard unit headword
#450 SE
Brief personal impressions are rarely __________, especially when recalled after many years; but the author's initial impression of Gellner as an exceptionally honest thinker is beyond reasonable doubt.Brief personal impressions are rarely __________, especially when recalled after many years; but the author's initial impression of Gellner as an exceptionally honest thinker is beyond reasonable doubt.
答案：B, E　definitive; authoritative
lasting
lasting: standard unit headword
#451 SE
The wealth of insightful and challenging articles found in the journal is astounding in a world where mainstream publications increasingly focus on the most __________ of topics.The wealth of insightful and challenging articles found in the journal is astounding in a world where mainstream publications increasingly focus on the most __________ of topics.
答案：A, C　hackneyed; pedestrian
prominent
prominent: standard unit headword
#452 TC TRIPLE
Flaherty understands that biological theories of artistic creation may (i) __________ people who cherish the idea that art comes from something other than the action of neurotransmitters, and she spends much time trying to shepherd us through the difficulty of accepting that the mind is actually the brain, a physical organ. She thinks we can (ii) __________ this without (iii) __________ the more exalted concepts of inspiration, the "inner voice," and so on. She, too, believes in those things.Flaherty understands that biological theories of artistic creation may (i) __________ people who cherish the idea that art comes from something other than the action of neurotransmitters, and she spends much time trying to shepherd us through the difficulty of accepting that the mind is actually the brain, a physical organ. She thinks we can (ii) __________ this without (iii) __________ the more exalted concepts of inspiration, the "inner voice," and so on. She, too, believes in those things.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　dismay; comprehend; discarding
appeal
appeal: standard unit headword
#453 SE
Critics have universally recognized Toni Morrison for __________ the limited perspectives of mainstream United States history by reclaiming the narratives of African American history, particularly from a female point of view.Critics have universally recognized Toni Morrison for __________ the limited perspectives of mainstream United States history by reclaiming the narratives of African American history, particularly from a female point of view.
答案：B, F　correcting; redressing
espouse
espouse: standard unit headword
#454 TC TRIPLE
The book's notoriety at the time it was published definitely had as a contributory factor its author's (i) __________, which meant that contemporary readers and critics were (ii) __________ the author's intellectual pedigree and motives. Hence it was also difficult, if not impossible, for the critics to (iii) __________ the book's arguments by engaging in ad hominem attacks. Readers repeatedly sought to discover the book's true provenance.The book's notoriety at the time it was published definitely had as a contributory factor its author's (i) __________, which meant that contemporary readers and critics were (ii) __________ the author's intellectual pedigree and motives. Hence it was also difficult, if not impossible, for the critics to (iii) __________ the book's arguments by engaging in ad hominem attacks. Readers repeatedly sought to discover the book's true provenance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　anonymity; unable to establish; dismiss
affluence
affluence: standard unit headword
#455 TC TRIPLE
Some critics say that the prime minister (i) __________ her positions too easily, but her supporters claim that she is merely (ii) __________ rather than quixotic and adaptable rather than (iii) __________.Some critics say that the prime minister (i) __________ her positions too easily, but her supporters claim that she is merely (ii) __________ rather than quixotic and adaptable rather than (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　changes; pragmatic; capricious
premature
premature: standard unit headword
#456 SE
When Ms. Alvarez campaigns, she tends to __________ small towns; most of her campaign appearances occur in large population centers and media markets.When Ms. Alvarez campaigns, she tends to __________ small towns; most of her campaign appearances occur in large population centers and media markets.
答案：B, D　eschew; shun
castigate
castigate: standard unit headword
#457 SE
Isaac Newton's career, which spanned nearly seven decades and continued to generate new results through the 1690s, was truly __________ and can therefore hardly be expected to exhibit a single continuous strand of development.Isaac Newton's career, which spanned nearly seven decades and continued to generate new results through the 1690s, was truly __________ and can therefore hardly be expected to exhibit a single continuous strand of development.
答案：B, F　multifarious; protean
illustrious
illustrious: standard unit headword
#458 TC TRIPLE
Despite the occasional (i)__________ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)__________ conference, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.Despite the occasional (i)__________ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)__________ conference, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　opulence; ascetic; respite from
venueallusion
venue: standard unit headword | allusion: standard unit headword
#459 SE
Excessive focus on what might have been can cause in us feelings of restlessness and regret, but some scientists are beginning to think that fancying an alternative reality might have __________ effects as well.Excessive focus on what might have been can cause in us feelings of restlessness and regret, but some scientists are beginning to think that fancying an alternative reality might have __________ effects as well.
答案：C, F　restorative; tonic
alternativeadverse
alternative: standard unit headword | adverse: standard unit headword
#460 TC TRIPLE
Because the term "memoir," unlike the term "autobiography," can suggest subjectivity rather than objectivity, Barton, in choosing to call his book a memoir, (i) __________ the accusation that his story may be (ii) __________. As Barton himself has noted, memoir promises only to give the reader an account of the author's memory, and no one but the author can therefore (iii) __________.Because the term "memoir," unlike the term "autobiography," can suggest subjectivity rather than objectivity, Barton, in choosing to call his book a memoir, (i) __________ the accusation that his story may be (ii) __________. As Barton himself has noted, memoir promises only to give the reader an account of the author's memory, and no one but the author can therefore (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　deflects; untrue; gauge its veracity

term: standard unit headword | appeal: standard unit headword
